contract.goal: Ship the zero-content jelly meta-plugin that cascade-installs the full jelly substrate family (commons, jelly-guild, jelly-loom, jelly-run), mirroring the existing agent-loop-full meta-bundle.
acceptance_criteria: plugins/jelly/.claude-plugin/plugin.json — {name: 'jelly', description} naming it a zero-content meta-bundle that cascades commons + jelly-guild + jelly-loom + jelly-run. No version field; no other files in the dir.
.claude-plugin/marketplace.json — jelly entry: source ./plugins/jelly, dependencies [commons, jelly-guild, jelly-loom, jelly-run] (commons first).
plugins/commons/cli/marketplace-manifest.test.ts — 'jelly' added to EXPECTED_PLUGIN_NAMES + a dependency-edges assertion (jelly deps == the four) + jelly added to the substrate-first ordering loop.
rules_applied: PLAN.md § Phase 2.2 Exit (plugins/jelly/ zero-content plugin.json + marketplace entry; deps cascade commons + jelly-guild + jelly-loom + jelly-run; claude plugin install jelly -> all four enabled).
Mirror the agent-loop-full meta-bundle: a single .claude-plugin/plugin.json (name + description, no version), with the cascade declared in the MARKETPLACE entry's dependencies, not the plugin.json.
Family-shape: minimal plugin.json (no version), commons-first dependency ordering.
Whiteboard skipped: project-local glob empty + a zero-content registration plugin does not warrant a design panel.

Panel: contract-fit + test-unit (substrate-only config + a structural test change; no domain evaluators). Both approved. Fixed one advisory (a pre-existing stale 'eight expected plugins' label -> count-agnostic) while in the file.
disqualifiers: Any content under plugins/jelly/ beyond plugin.json (it is a zero-content bundle).
A dependency set that does not cascade the full jelly family (commons + jelly-guild + jelly-loom + jelly-run).

actions: Wrote plugins/jelly/.claude-plugin/plugin.json: {name: 'jelly', description} — zero-content meta-bundle, no version. The dir contains only this file (verified via find).

Added the jelly entry to .claude-plugin/marketplace.json (after jelly-run, keeping the jelly family contiguous): source ./plugins/jelly, dependencies [commons, jelly-guild, jelly-loom, jelly-run] (commons first).

Updated plugins/commons/cli/marketplace-manifest.test.ts: added 'jelly' to EXPECTED_PLUGIN_NAMES (drives the per-entry describe loop — source resolves, plugin.json exists + name matches); added a 'jelly meta-bundle cascade-installs the full jelly family' edge test (sorted toEqual == [commons, jelly-guild, jelly-loom, jelly-run], mirroring the agent-loop-full sibling); added 'jelly' to the substrate-first ordering loop (commons first); made the stale 'declares exactly the eight expected plugins' label count-agnostic.

Verified: find plugins/jelly -> only .claude-plugin/plugin.json (zero-content). npx vitest run -> 80 files, 1200 passed (was 1188; +12). marketplace-manifest test file: 118 passed. Panel (contract-fit + test-unit) both approved.

notes_for_pr: Test plan: the empirical exit criterion — `claude plugin install jelly` on a fresh ~/.claude/ resulting in all four plugins (commons + jelly-guild + jelly-loom + jelly-run) enabled — is a real install that mutates ~/.claude and must be run manually by the operator (per the PLAN's own mitigation). The automated marketplace-manifest tripwire validates the cascade DECLARATION (deps resolve within the marketplace, commons-first ordering, the exact edge set); the install itself is the manual check.
signal: phase-boundary: U1 is the only + last unit in Phase 2.2 (single signal; no revise offered).

Phase 2.2 complete: the jelly substrate family is fully built (jelly-guild + jelly-loom + jelly-run + the jelly meta-bundle). The remaining phase is 3.1 — dogfood against a real workstream — which needs the operator to pick a topic.
